Use this skill when shipping, releasing, or reviewing deploy configuration.

## Concepts (timeless)
- Build once, promote the same artifact across environments; configuration comes from the environment, not the build.
- Make releases reversible: keep the previous version deployable and have a rehearsed rollback.
- Roll out progressively (canary/blue-green) for risky changes; watch health signals before full cutover.
- Migrations are forward-compatible and reversible; decouple schema changes from code that requires them.
- Health checks, readiness gates, and observability are part of "done", not follow-ups.

## Pull current docs first
Platform + orchestration APIs change frequently — confirm the current docs for THIS target:
- your deploy platform + orchestrator's current docs

## Checklist
- [ ] Immutable artifact promoted across envs; config externalized
- [ ] Rollback path exists and is rehearsed
- [ ] Risky changes rolled out progressively with health gates
- [ ] Migrations forward-compatible + reversible
- [ ] Health/readiness checks + observability wired before cutover
